economic_notes: Nation receives 47% of revenues from ISC. Development Corporation includes Buffalo Point Development Corp. Ltd. and 5246831 Manitoba Limited. Nation operates marina, restaurant, golf course, accommodations, VLT, and property tax programs. COVID-19 loan of $150,000 received with $70,000 forgivable portion.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Nation is in Manitoba (MB). Consolidated entity includes Buffalo Point First Nation Housing Authority, Buffalo Point Development Corp. Ltd., and Buffalo Point First Nation itself. Deferred revenue is very large ($9.78M) primarily due to long-term lot lease prepayments (up to 70-year leases) recognized over lease life. Promissory note of $198,623 to Receiver General is interest-free and past maturity date with renewal pending. Contaminated sites exist but no liability recorded. Capital lease obligations of $213,493 excluded from long-term debt figure shown. ISC funding calculated as ISC Health ($1,905,064) + ISC ($1,762,890) = $3,667,954. Total government transfers include ISC Health + ISC + CMHC + TLEC = $3,913,288. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text. Annual debt service reflects long-term debt repayments only; capital lease repayments were additional $61,111.
